content: Bloodstream infections (BSI) are classified as primary (no identifiable source) or secondary (from focus like pneumonia, UTI, intra-abdominal). Catheter-related bloodstream infection (CRBSI) requires: positive blood culture from catheter AND clinical signs of infection (fever, chills, hypotension) AND no other apparent source. Central line-associated BSI (CLABSI) is CDC surveillance definition: laboratory-confirmed BSI in patient with central line within 48 hours, not related to infection at another site. Common pathogens: coagulase-negative staphylococci (especially catheter-associated), S. aureus, Enterobacteriaceae, Candida species, Pseudomonas. Risk factors: central venous catheters, total parenteral nutrition, hemodialysis, immunosuppression, prolonged hospitalization. Mortality 15-25%, higher with multidrug-resistant organisms and delayed appropriate therapy.
